summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #297, #298, #299 — the framework-agnostic core of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue foundation in ars-core, delivered as a single combined PR.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented and pushed as PR #698 (commit range initial → 9efae941+), CI green after a coverage fix. Nine rounds of Codex review were run (45 findings addressed), with pass 9 pushed and awaiting Codex's next verdict before merge.

repo: ars-core is no_std; transcendental float math must use core_maths::CoreFloat in fully-qualified form (CoreFloat::powf(x, e)) — never method syntax, which silently compiles but does nothing in no_std. CoreFloat lacks min/max; add private fmin/fmax helpers.
MessageFn has a fixed set of From<F> impls keyed on exact fn-pointer arity/type; any new message signature requires a new hand-written From<F> impl in crates/ars-core/src/message_fn.rs.

The adapter-driven drag pattern: core handles Idle↔Dragging state and value math; the adapter fires DragStart/DragMove/DragEnd. Core fires a typed Effect::ChangeEnd on DragEnd — never call a nonexistent use_platform_effects() or track_pointer_drag() inside Machine::transition.

Bindable<T>::get() returns the controlled (parent-supplied) value; pending() returns the staged internal value. All position, ARIA, display, and hidden-input rendering must use pending() during and after user interaction — using get() causes controlled-mode display to lag one commit behind.

ColorValue::new wraps hue into [0,360). A Hue-channel slider/wheel reaching its max endpoint wraps to 0 and jumps the thumb back to min. Fix: track a separate unwrapped slider_value / hue_value for ARIA and position; derive the normalized ColorValue from it.

The workspace denies clippy::suspicious (workspace-level Cargo.toml lint). A pre-existing pagination::mod.rs map_or_else lint became a CI blocker when stable toolchain updated to 1.96.0 — the fix is a 1-line unwrap_or_else substitution.

The spec-compile-snippets gate compiles spec code blocks in isolation but does not require them to exactly match the implementation. Spec drift (e.g. nonexistent use_platform_effects) is doc-accuracy, not a CI gate — reconcile via spec edits, not impl workarounds.

snapshot-count gate (part of xci-fast) requires ≥1 insta snapshot per declared state variant. AngleSlider has 3 states (Idle/Dragging/Focused) so needs ≥3 snapshots; missing a state causes xci-fast failure.

CI coverage uses cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace, which runs cross-crate tests that exercise ars-components and boosts branch coverage ~1.1% above a -p-only run. Always verify with --workspace before pushing to avoid a false-pass locally.

DragEnd must bypass the disabled/readonly guard so an in-flight drag can always terminate cleanly. Only value-mutation events (DragStart, DragMove, keyboard) should be blocked; focus/blur/drag-end must fall through.

RTL horizontal controls must invert the normalized drag position (1.0 - pos) in apply_slider_position/apply_area_position, not just flip arrow key direction. Both pointer inversion and render mirroring are required for full RTL correctness.

Props::readonly should set HtmlAttr::ReadOnly (native readonly attr) in addition to aria-readonly, and the machine's Change/blur-commit arms must check ctx.readonly. The IME CompositionStart early-return branch runs before the readonly guard — it needs its own ctx.readonly check.
color_swatch_picker focused_index must be gated on State::Focused when rendering item data-ars-focused; otherwise blur leaves the stale index styled as focused while the root is idle.
Hidden form inputs must be disabled when ctx.disabled || ctx.invalid so the browser omits them from form submission. An invalid ColorField must not submit its stale color value.

code.finding: ColorValue::new must coerce non-finite components to 0.0 before wrap/clamp; debug_assert alone doesn't protect release builds from NaN propagating into CSS custom properties and ARIA attributes.

from_hex used trim_start_matches('#') which strips ALL leading '#' chars; use strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) to strip at most one. Also guard with hex.is_ascii() before byte-indexing — non-ASCII input (e.g. #ああ) panics at non-char-boundary.
parse_rgb_args/parse_hsl_args/parse_hsb_args accepted NaN/infinity because parse::<f64>() succeeds on those strings. Add a parse_finite_f64 helper and use it in all numeric parse branches.

Bindable<T> exposes pending() returning &T (the staged internal value) vs get() which returns the controlled prop. pending() was added as part of this session; it is the correct source for all display, ARIA, and hidden-input rendering in controlled mode.

Four new From<F> impls were added for color message signatures: Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,f64,&Locale), Fn(&str,&str,&Locale) (slider valuetext), Fn(&str,&str,&str,&Locale) (area valuetext). Each arity requires its own impl block; the compiler will not coerce closures otherwise.

apply_slider_position must invert normalized position (1.0 - pos) for horizontal RTL. track_attrs alpha gradient first stop must use the same HSL color at alpha=0 (not CSS 'transparent' which is transparent black) to avoid the gradient fading through black for non-black colors.

Percentage channels (saturation, lightness, brightness, alpha) display in 0-100 range. commit_input must divide the parsed number by 100 before clamping to channel range [0,1], and format_value must multiply by 100. Also aria-valuenow/min/max must be scaled the same way for correct spinbutton semantics.

SetToMax wrote hue=360 directly into ColorValue bypassing new() normalization, violating the [0,360) invariant. Fix: track unwrapped hue_value (like slider_value in color_slider) for ARIA/display; derive color from it. Also Increment/Decrement used % instead of rem_euclid, producing negative remainders on large_step > current hue.
Wheel thumb set aria-labelledby to a *-label element ID, but ColorWheel has no Label part — the IDREF dangled. Drop aria-labelledby; aria-label is already set directly on the thumb.

SetProps updated min/max but left ctx.value unaffected, allowing out-of-range values to persist in aria-valuenow and the hidden input. Re-clamp the value to [new_min, new_max] inside the SetProps arm.
FocusUp/FocusDown compute row offset using columns. If columns=0 the row delta is 0 and navigation is a no-op. Clamp columns to max(1, props.columns) before row arithmetic.

In idle state focused_index is None and every item got tabindex=-1, making the listbox unreachable by keyboard. Add tabbable_index() = selected_index or 0 and give that item tabindex=0 in idle state for roving entry.

workflow: Spec-sync agents can be run in parallel with test/build work: delegate 'mirror implementation changes back to spec files' as a subagent while running cargo tests locally, then validate and push together. Saves meaningful wall-clock time per round.

For Codex review loops: post @codex review, poll for 👀 reaction (Codex starting), then poll for 👀 dropping + unresolved threads (Codex done). Reply to each thread with the fix + test name, resolve via the GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation, then re-trigger. Use a background watcher script rather than sleeping the main loop.
zsh treats 'status' as a read-only special variable. Background poll scripts must use a different variable name (e.g. cov_state) or they crash silently with exit code 1.

cargo xci-fast runs 10 gates sequentially; the snapshot-count gate runs late and caught a missing state-variant snapshot (AngleSlider Idle-root) that would otherwise have been a CI failure. Always run xci-fast locally before pushing.

TDD discipline per finding: write the failing test first, confirm it fails, implement the fix, confirm it passes, then move to the next finding. This pattern caught several mistaken assertions (wrong channel range, wrong snapshot value) before they became noise.
When Codex posts a re-raise of a finding already fixed in a prior push (stale anchor), reply citing the exact test name that covers it and resolve immediately rather than re-implementing.

friction: core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min() and max(), requiring private fmin/fmax helpers — this gap wasn't documented anywhere and required a failed build + source inspection to discover.
The spec described APIs that don't exist in the codebase (use_platform_effects(), track_pointer_drag() in core), requiring significant reconciliation work before any code could be written.

MessageFn From<F> impls are silently missing for new signatures — the compiler error ('closure doesn't satisfy the bound') only appears at the call site, not at the definition, making it hard to diagnose the first time.

ars-components branch coverage fell to 89.7% after adding the color modules, requiring a full second round of test-writing to close the one-armed if-flag conditional branches. The 'measure with --workspace not -p' discrepancy wasted time calibrating thresholds.
Codex was rate-limited ('reached your usage limits for code reviews') for the entire first review trigger, blocking the review loop until quota reset with no ETA.
zsh read-only 'status' variable silently crashed two background watcher scripts (exit code 1, misleading summary), requiring variable rename to diagnose.
The spec agent occasionally reformatted YAML frontmatter (related: array) into multi-line block style that failed spec validate. Required a follow-up fix pass.

Controlled-mode pending/get() inconsistency was pervasive across all 6 stateful machines — Codex surfaced it in waves across 4 passes (passes 7, 8, 9) because each machine had the same class of bug in slightly different forms.
The Bash tool's safety classifier was temporarily unavailable mid-session (pass 6), blocking the reply/resolve/re-trigger step and requiring a retry after a brief wait.
open_items: PR #698 is not yet merged — awaiting Codex 👍 (pass 9 just pushed, pass 10 verdict pending) and explicit user approval to merge. Issues #297/#298/#299 close automatically on merge.
Adapter components (spec/components/leptos|dioxus/specialized/color-*.md) describe future Leptos/Dioxus adapter work — explicitly out of scope for #297–299, should be tracked as a follow-up epic.

ColorValue spec location: the type lives in ars-core/src/color.rs but is documented inside color-picker.md §Types. Promoting it to spec/shared/color-types.md with its own manifest entry would improve discoverability — orthogonal to correctness, deferred.

MessageFn foundation spec (spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md) representative listing omits the 4 color-specific From<F> signatures added in this session — the listing is intentionally non-exhaustive but leaving it inconsistent adds confusion for future contributors.

The Codex chatgpt-codex-connector bot quota exhaustion pattern: the first two @codex review triggers returned 'reached usage limits'. If this recurs, the review-loop skill should detect this response and schedule a retry rather than parking the loop forever.
